items.title: Indian payments chief thinks AI will be heavily involved in next era of digital payment growth
items.author: Ivan Mehta
items.content: <article>Dilip Asbe said that newer UPI apps could be more competitive with a viable commercial model</article>

items.title: Instagram is testing more ways to customize ‘Your Algorithm’
items.author: Anthony Ha
items.content: <article>Instagram users could soon see more ways to tune their content.</article>

items.title: SoftBank’s CEO isn’t the only one with questions about Elon Musk’s orbital data center hype
items.author: Anthony Ha
items.content: <article>Not everyone is buying Elon Musk’s vision for orbital data centers.</article>

items.title: Apple Vision Pro exec is reportedly leaving for OpenAI
items.author: Anthony Ha
items.content: <article>Paul Meade, the Apple vice president in charge of the Vision Pro headset, is reportedly leaving the company to join OpenAI’s hardware team.</article>

items.title: The fittest founder in the room got cancer. Here’s how he used AI to fight back.
items.author: Connie Loizos
items.content: <article>When confronted with cancer, Connor Christou fed everything tied tied to his regime — blood results, scan data, wearable output, journal entries — into Claude. </article>

items.title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on
items.author: Kate Park
items.content: <article>New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban. U.S. AI labs may never recover this enormous market.</article>

items.title: Trump Admin releases Anthropic Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
items.author: Julie Bort
items.content: <article>Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.</article>

items.title: FTC gives Musk the OK to acquire SpaceX alumni startup Mesh
items.author: Marina Temkin
items.content: <article>Mesh came out of stealth in February with a $50 million Series A. </article>

items.title: Corgi, the buzzy Y Combinator-backed insurance tech startup, says it didn’t steal an open source product
items.author: Julie Bort
items.content: <article>Corgi became embroiled in  controversy when Papermark accused it of stealing its software. Corgi says it did not, raising new questions about vibe coding.</article>

items.title: Novak Djokovic has a new job — advisor to private equity firm General Atlantic
items.author: Dominic-Madori Davis
items.content: <article>General Atlantic has tapped tennis legend Novak Djokovic to serve as a global strategic advisor. </article>

items.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
items.author: Rebecca Bellan
items.content: <article>“We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.”</article>

items.title: OpenAI poaches Uber India chief to lead its biggest market outside the US
items.author: Jagmeet Singh
items.content: <article>The hire marks OpenAI's latest push into India, expanding offices, partnerships and hiring.</article>

items.title: Why everyone from OpenAI to SpaceX is building their own chips (and turning up the heat on Nvidia)
items.author: Theresa Loconsolo
items.content: <article>Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending.   OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built with Broadcom, joining Google, Apple, and SpaceX in a growing list of companies building their way out of single-supplier risk. The goal is less of a […]</article>

items.title: Russian hackers were behind $2.5B hack of Jaguar Land Rover: Report
items.author: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
items.content: <article>The hack on car giant Jaguar Land Rover last year was one of the most disrupting, damaging, and costly hacks of the last few years.</article>

items.title: Tesla settles FSD crash lawsuit as federal investigations continue
items.author: Kirsten Korosec
items.content: <article>The lawsuit was connected to a fatal 2023 crash involving a vehicle using the company's advanced driver assistance system known as Full Self-Driving.</article>

items.title: TikTok’s road to becoming a super app
items.author: Aisha Malik
items.content: <article>TikTok may be working to become the app that people use for most of their digital activities.</article>

items.title: It’s not about Anthropic vs. OpenAI anymore
items.author: Russell Brandom
items.content: <article>AI models have progressed to the point where their capabilities have real political consequences. Dealing with those consequences will require collective action.</article>

items.title: Xprize founder says ‘humans behave better when they’re being watched’
items.author: Sean O'Kane
items.content: <article>Peter Diamandis is the latest tech executive to argue that global surveillance will make the world a better place, following Larry Ellison's comments in 2024.</article>

items.title: Early Bird pricing ends tonight for TechCrunch Founder Summit
items.author: TechCrunch Events
items.content: <article>Save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026. Early Bird pricing ends today, at 11:59 p.m. PT, after which rates increase. Register now.</article>

items.title: Robotaxis drive miles just to get cleaned and charged; this new startup wants to fix that
items.author: Kirsten Korosec
items.content: <article>Aseon Labs, which came out of Y Combinator's 2026 spring cohort, has raised $10 million from Crane Venture Partners and others. </article>
